service_name: Information Security Strategy and Planning Services

capability_questions.capability_name: Service exists and is in good condition

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a strategy and planning service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the strategy and planning service exists
questions.evaluation_focus: Documented service or function charter

questions.prompt: Is there a documented Strategy and Planning Service Description?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the service description is documented
questions.evaluation_focus: Documented service or function charter

questions.prompt: Is the service adopted enterprise-wide?
questions.intent: Evaluate enterprise-wide adoption of the service
questions.evaluation_focus: Operating model and delivery cadence

questions.prompt: Is the service audited or reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether the service is audited or reviewed on a regular cadence
questions.evaluation_focus: Stakeholder satisfaction and continuous improvement

questions.prompt: As part of strategy and planning, does the enterprise have an information security program?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a formal information security program exists
questions.evaluation_focus: Documented program definition or charter

questions.prompt: Who leads the information security program and how is it reported into the organization?
questions.intent: Evaluate program leadership and reporting line
questions.evaluation_focus: Program leadership and reporting line

questions.prompt: What is the security vision of the enterprise?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a security vision is defined
questions.evaluation_focus: Published security vision

questions.prompt: Is the security vision reviewed once every five years?
questions.intent: Evaluate security vision review cadence
questions.evaluation_focus: Published security vision

questions.prompt: What are the mission statements that enable the enterprise to achieve the security vision?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether security mission statements exist and support the vision
questions.evaluation_focus: Published security mission

questions.prompt: Is the efficacy of mission statements reviewed annually?
questions.intent: Evaluate annual review of mission statement efficacy
questions.evaluation_focus: Communication and alignment with enterprise strategy

questions.prompt: Who is the sponsor or leader for the strategy and planning service?
questions.intent: Evaluate identification of the security sponsor or service leader
questions.evaluation_focus: Named executive sponsor for the program

questions.prompt: How often does the security sponsor engage with security leadership on program priorities, funding, and escalations?
questions.intent: Evaluate sponsor engagement and accountability for the program
questions.evaluation_focus: Regular sponsor engagement with security leadership

questions.prompt: Is there a dedicated team that manages the strategic decisions relevant to the business, specifically security?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a dedicated strategy and planning team exists
questions.evaluation_focus: Team or role assignment for strategy and planning

questions.prompt: How does the strategy and planning team work with security operations, risk, and business stakeholders?
questions.intent: Evaluate collaboration between the planning team and stakeholders
questions.evaluation_focus: Collaboration with security operations and business stakeholders

capability_questions.capability_name: Security strategy alignment with business objectives

questions.prompt: How is your information security strategy linked to business objectives and risk appetite today?
questions.intent: Evaluate linkage between security strategy and business objectives
questions.evaluation_focus: Linkage to business objectives and risk appetite

questions.prompt: How often is the security strategy reviewed and updated in line with enterprise planning cycles?
questions.intent: Evaluate security strategy refresh cadence
questions.evaluation_focus: Refresh cadence tied to business planning

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a current 3-year or 5-year roadmap in place for IT security enhancement and adoption?
questions.intent: Evaluate existence of a 3- or 5-year information security adoption roadmap
questions.evaluation_focus: Horizons including 3-year and 5-year planning views

questions.prompt: Does the roadmap cover the entire enterprise, subsidiaries, and all physical locations including cloud segments?
questions.intent: Evaluate enterprise-wide scope of the security roadmap
questions.evaluation_focus: Roadmap for the information security program

questions.prompt: How at least quarterly is the roadmap reviewed (semi-annually or annually)?
questions.intent: Evaluate roadmap review frequency
questions.evaluation_focus: Initiative prioritization, dependencies, and change control

questions.prompt: How are roadmap initiatives prioritized, and how do you manage dependencies, constraints, and changes over time?
questions.intent: Evaluate roadmap prioritization and change control
questions.evaluation_focus: Initiative prioritization, dependencies, and change control

questions.prompt: What governance bodies or forums own security strategy decisions, and what decisions do they make?
questions.intent: Evaluate security governance forums and decision scope
questions.evaluation_focus: Security governance forum or equivalent

questions.prompt: How are accountabilities documented (e.g., RACI) for strategy, risk acceptance, and funding?
questions.intent: Evaluate documented accountability for strategy decisions
questions.evaluation_focus: RACI for strategy decisions

questions.prompt: Are governance bodies (committees, councils, or boards) defined with clear responsibilities and decision scope for security governance?
questions.intent: Evaluate defined governance bodies and decision scope
questions.evaluation_focus: Defined governance bodies, responsibilities, and decision scope

questions.prompt: Is there a documented RACI for key security governance decisions, with separation of duties from infrastructure and operations owners?
questions.intent: Evaluate documented RACI for security governance decisions
questions.evaluation_focus: Documented RACI for key security decisions

questions.prompt: Are escalation paths defined for high-risk governance decisions, policy exceptions, and risk acceptance?
questions.intent: Evaluate escalation paths for high-risk decisions and exceptions
questions.evaluation_focus: Clear escalation paths for high-risk decisions and exceptions

questions.prompt: Is there a named governance leader with a clear mandate and delegated authority for enterprise security governance?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether a named governance leader exists with mandate
questions.evaluation_focus: Named leader with clear mandate and delegated authority

questions.prompt: Does the governance leader engage executives and delivery stakeholders through a defined engagement model?
questions.intent: Evaluate engagement model with executives and delivery stakeholders
questions.evaluation_focus: Engagement model with executives and delivery stakeholders

questions.prompt: How are cyber and information security risks represented in your enterprise risk register?
questions.intent: Evaluate integration of security risks into enterprise risk management
questions.evaluation_focus: Risk taxonomy alignment

questions.prompt: Describe your process for risk treatment plans, owners, and tracking to closure.
questions.intent: Evaluate risk treatment planning and tracking
questions.evaluation_focus: Risk treatment plans and owners

questions.prompt: How are security policies and standards structured, owned, and kept current?
questions.intent: Evaluate policy and standards framework maturity
questions.evaluation_focus: Policy hierarchy and ownership

questions.prompt: What is your process for policy exceptions and how are exceptions tracked?
questions.intent: Evaluate policy exception governance
questions.evaluation_focus: Review and exception process

questions.prompt: How is the security budget planned and tied to strategic initiatives on your roadmap?
questions.intent: Evaluate budget linkage to the security roadmap
questions.evaluation_focus: Budget linkage to roadmap

questions.prompt: How do you plan for security staffing, skills gaps, and managed service needs?
questions.intent: Evaluate security resource and capacity planning
questions.evaluation_focus: Skills and capacity planning

questions.prompt: Are there documented KPIs, KRIs, or other metrics for the strategy and planning service?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether KPIs and KRIs are documented for the service
questions.evaluation_focus: Defined KPIs and KRIs

questions.prompt: Are the KPIs and KRIs reviewed at least quarterly (quarterly, semi-annually, or annually) based on the type of metric?
questions.intent: Evaluate KPI and KRI review cadence
questions.evaluation_focus: Reporting cadence to leadership

questions.prompt: Give an example where KPIs or KRIs changed a strategic or funding decision.
questions.intent: Evaluate use of KPIs and KRIs in leadership decisions
questions.evaluation_focus: Use of metrics in decisions

questions.prompt: What KPIs and KRIs are documented to measure enterprise security governance outcomes (for example policy exception rate, decision cycle time, or oversight completeness)?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether governance KPIs and KRIs are documented
questions.evaluation_focus: Defined KPIs/KRIs tied to governance outcomes

questions.prompt: Are governance metrics reported to executive leadership and the board (or equivalent oversight body) on a defined cadence?
questions.intent: Evaluate leadership and board reporting cadence for governance metrics
questions.evaluation_focus: Leadership and board reporting cadence

questions.prompt: Are governance metrics reviewed at least quarterly and used to prioritize investments and risk decisions?
questions.intent: Evaluate whether metrics inform decisions and prioritization
questions.evaluation_focus: Evidence that metrics inform decisions and prioritization

questions.prompt: Where DORA or similar regulatory obligations apply, are ICT risk governance and board oversight metrics tracked and reported?
questions.intent: Evaluate DORA and regulatory governance reporting metrics
questions.evaluation_focus: DORA and regulatory governance reporting metrics where applicable

questions.prompt: How do you maintain an inventory of regulatory, contractual, and assurance obligations?
questions.intent: Evaluate compliance obligations inventory
questions.evaluation_focus: Compliance obligations inventory

questions.prompt: How are controls and roadmap items mapped to compliance obligations?
questions.intent: Evaluate mapping of controls and roadmap items to obligations
questions.evaluation_focus: Control mapping to obligations

capability_questions.capability_name: Third-party and supply chain security strategy

questions.prompt: How does your security strategy address third-party and supply chain risk?
questions.intent: Evaluate third-party and supply chain security strategy
questions.evaluation_focus: Tiering or criticality model

questions.prompt: How are critical vendors tiered, assessed, and monitored over time?
questions.intent: Evaluate vendor tiering, assessment, and monitoring
questions.evaluation_focus: Ongoing monitoring approach

questions.prompt: What workforce security awareness and training plans support your strategy?
questions.intent: Evaluate workforce security training plans
questions.evaluation_focus: Role-based training plan

questions.prompt: How do you measure security culture or human-risk outcomes?
questions.intent: Evaluate measurement of security culture outcomes
questions.evaluation_focus: Phishing or culture metrics

questions.prompt: Is there a governed user segmentation strategy with defined criteria (roles, privilege tiers, risk levels) applied to identity technologies such as Active Directory or cloud identity providers?
questions.intent: Evaluate user segmentation criteria and identity governance
questions.evaluation_focus: Defined user segmentation criteria and governed identity categories

questions.prompt: Is there a governed workload micro-segmentation strategy defining principles and target scope for applications and services (including policy-as-code, agents, or service mesh)?
questions.intent: Evaluate micro-segmentation principles and workload scope
questions.evaluation_focus: Defined micro-segmentation principles and workload scope

questions.prompt: Is there a governed network macro-segmentation strategy defining zones, allowable traffic patterns, and control enforcement (firewalls, NAC, cloud segmentation)?
questions.intent: Evaluate network segmentation zones and traffic patterns
questions.evaluation_focus: Defined network segmentation zones and allowable traffic patterns

questions.prompt: Are user, workload, and network segmentation strategies formalized, documented, and implemented enterprise-wide—not only in select locations informally?
questions.intent: Evaluate enterprise-wide formalization of segmentation strategies
questions.evaluation_focus: Enterprise-wide formalization and implementation of segmentation strategies

questions.prompt: Is exception governance and effectiveness measurement defined for segmentation strategies (including drift monitoring where applicable)?
questions.intent: Evaluate exception governance and segmentation effectiveness measurement
questions.evaluation_focus: Exception governance and measurement of segmentation effectiveness

questions.prompt: Are segmentation governance strategies reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy and alignment to policy requirements?
questions.intent: Evaluate quarterly review of segmentation governance strategies
questions.evaluation_focus: Quarterly review of segmentation governance strategies

questions.prompt: Does enterprise security governance use independent expertise or benchmarks (threat intelligence, advisory, peer benchmarks) to inform policy and compliance initiatives?
questions.intent: Evaluate use of external expertise and benchmarks in governance
questions.evaluation_focus: Use of independent expertise or benchmarks to inform governance

questions.prompt: Is there evidence of periodic external review or validation of governance direction (for example third-party assurance or industry benchmarking)?
questions.intent: Evaluate periodic external review or validation
questions.evaluation_focus: Evidence of periodic external review or validation

questions.prompt: Is there a clear mechanism to incorporate external findings—including AI-era threat research—into governance decisions and improvements?
questions.intent: Evaluate mechanism to incorporate external findings into governance
questions.evaluation_focus: Clear mechanism to incorporate findings into decisions and improvements

questions.prompt: Does the governance charter or operating model define oversight and accountability for AI systems used in or affecting the enterprise?
questions.intent: Evaluate governance charter for AI system oversight
questions.evaluation_focus: Governance charter addresses AI system oversight and accountability

questions.prompt: Are EU AI Act and sector-specific AI compliance obligations reflected in security governance scope and decision rights?
questions.intent: Evaluate EU AI Act obligations in governance scope
questions.evaluation_focus: EU AI Act and sector AI compliance obligations reflected in governance scope

questions.prompt: Where DORA applies, is ICT risk governance integrated into security governance committees and reviewed at least quarterly?
questions.intent: Evaluate DORA ICT risk governance integration
questions.evaluation_focus: DORA ICT risk governance integrated into security governance cadence
